root_cause_candidates.cause: Symptom service frontend exhibits loss fault but there is no error evidence in logs and no upstream references to trace a downstream/root cause. No deeper root cause can be confidently identified from the available data.
root_cause_candidates.reasoning: All sources converge on frontend as the symptom service, but none provide a credible deeper root cause. Log Agent reports insufficient error evidence; Topology shows plausible propagation from any dependency but does not pinpoint a root cause; TCB-RCA places frontend as a propagation target with no upstream anomaly timing. In absence of upstream errors, we cannot distinguish a deeper root cause, so frontend is the only defensible placeholder root given current data, with weak confidence.

metadata.llm_reasoning: Symptom service frontend depends on multiple downstream services. A root cause in any of these dependencies can propagate to frontend, causing the observed symptom. The topology provides multiple plausible one-hop propagation paths (each dependency to frontend). The blast radius currently includes only frontend, consistent with the given structure. Since frontend is the direct consumer of these services, the likelihood of propagation is highest along any of these dependency links; no alternative upstream paths exist in the topology beyond these direct dependencies.

result.synthesis_reasoning: There is no strong concordance indicating a deeper root cause. Log evidence is absent, topology permits multiple plausible one-hop propagation paths from various dependencies to frontend, and the TCB-RCA result is uninformative (frontend only). Given the UNOBSERVED root-cause detection rule, there is no identifiable upstream service with missing logs to elevate as a higher-probability root cause. Therefore, the analysis yields a weak convergence with frontend as the only defensible, though shallow, root-cause placeholder. Recommend increasing logging/telemetry on all frontend dependencies (cartservice, productcatalogservice, currencyservice, recommendationservice, checkoutservice, adservice, shippingservice) and implementing cross-service health checks to reveal any unobserved upstream failures.
